fingerprint.signature: Terminal input silently omits underscore, colon or pipe characters from long commands while the session remains connected.
stableConditions: The command is entered through the OrcaTerm browser terminal input path.
The intended command contains shell-sensitive punctuation or long environment bindings.
observedEvidence: The rendered server-side command differs from the intended command.
Repeating or repasting the same command does not prove input integrity.

rootCause.conclusion: The OrcaTerm browser input path is not a trustworthy transport for long shell commands with special characters.
evidence: Short alphanumeric launcher commands arrive intact.
Long environment-bound commands showed silent punctuation loss.
The short launcher reads exact values from a checksum-bound facts file.
The launcher revalidates the complete source set before any mutation.

regression.redCase: A manually entered long environment command can be silently changed before server execution.
evidence: The fixed-channel suite passes 13 of 13 cases.
A changed README or manifest byte is rejected before mutation.
The production host returned PASS_EXACT_INSTALL_PACKAGE_VERIFIED_NO_MUTATION through the short verify command.
The persistent timer and service are not installed yet.
Production verify-only passed for source commit 1411618d44eccd88b8714bf04df2a99a47f471dd.
Production install and post-install acceptance remain pending.

workaroundAccounting.unknownReason: Elapsed-time instrumentation was introduced after the repeated input failures had already occurred.

remainingRisks: The external vendor input implementation is not observable.
OrcaTerm remains necessary for the one-time fixed-channel bootstrap and cloud MFA exceptions.

fingerprint.signature: The OrcaTerm file manager immediately reports 0B of the expected size and the destination file is absent on the server.
stableConditions: The same source bytes fail from both a hidden cache path and a short visible path.
observedEvidence: Server sha256sum returned ENOENT after both failed uploads.
The host had 66GB free, ruling out target disk exhaustion.

rootCause.conclusion: The browser file-upload session was stale independently of the still-connected terminal session.
evidence: Changing source path length, visibility and extended attributes did not change the 0B failure.
Reloading the OrcaTerm site and reconnecting transferred the exact same 19156 bytes in one second.
The server-side SHA-256 matched 80e0932a64feb0347dd41a7e4361cc90d0e64497a526cc16ad58148317479cdd.
Routine non-secret packages move to an Ed25519 signed pull-only Git channel.
The production host will poll without browser upload sessions or inbound ports.

regression.redCase: A connected terminal can coexist with a stale upload session that transfers zero bytes.
regression.greenCommand: npm run test:recurrence-gate && npm run test:production-dispatch
evidence: The recurrence gate retires repeated OrcaTerm bundle transport.
The fixed channel validates signed exact bundles and rejects tampering.
The exact bootstrap archive uploaded only after session reload and reconnect.
The server-side package verify returned PASS_EXACT_INSTALL_PACKAGE_VERIFIED_NO_MUTATION.
The fixed pull-only timer is not installed yet.
The exact bootstrap archive exists on the Tencent production host with the expected SHA-256.
Persistent channel installation and one real signed dispatch acceptance remain pending.

workaroundAccounting.unknownReason: Elapsed-time instrumentation was introduced after the two controlled upload failures.

remainingRisks: Tencent does not expose the internal upload-session token state.
The browser upload path remains an emergency bootstrap dependency until the fixed timer passes production acceptance.
